jokes.text: Cramer's been dead for six months.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if one of them is Jim Cramer from Mad Money
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that the famous TV host has been dead for months
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivery - treats shocking news as mundane business detail. Economy and timing are flawless.

jokes.text: The Astors, the Rockefellers, the Sbarros...
jokes.setup: Listing New York royalty families
jokes.punchline: Including the pizza chain family as equals to actual aristocracy
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock high-low comedy mixing legitimate aristocracy with commercial brands. Perfect Jack character moment.

jokes.text: You think I don't want to know what Pizzarina Sbarro is going to be wearing?
jokes.setup: Previous Sbarro reference
jokes.punchline: Made-up aristocratic name combining pizza and marina
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the Sbarro joke with a perfectly pretentious fake name that sounds plausibly aristocratic.

jokes.text: I'm on Dodecacil, the pill where you only get your period once a year. We're so close to beating that thing completely.
jokes.setup: Explaining a medical excuse for the weekend
jokes.punchline: Treating menstruation like a disease to be conquered with militaristic language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Avery character moment - treating biological functions like corporate efficiency problems. The escalation from pill to 'beating that thing' is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: I promise you, this weekend will be filled with staring out windows while holding a glass of Scotch.
jokes.setup: Avery asking him to think about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Reducing deep emotional contemplation to a visual cliché
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - turning emotional vulnerability into performative masculinity. The specificity of the visual makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What's that supposed to mean, you dumb douche? Oh, it's starting.
jokes.setup: Jack says he'll see her in two weeks
jokes.punchline: Immediate mood swing blamed on her period starting
jokes.explanation: Pays off the earlier period setup with perfect timing. The contrast between sophisticated Avery and crude insult works well.

jokes.text: Aloha means hello and goodbye. So, aloha.
jokes.setup: Awkward goodbye moment
jokes.punchline: Using Hawaiian greeting to avoid commitment to hello or goodbye
jokes.explanation: Clever use of linguistic ambiguity to reflect relationship uncertainty. Perfectly economical delivery.

jokes.text: We'll just do it together on that tour boat that's painted like a shark.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they spend time together thinking
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific tourist attraction as romantic venue
jokes.explanation: The specificity of 'painted like a shark' elevates what could be generic tourist humor. Nancy's earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But I would still like two meals.
jokes.setup: Confirming she's coming alone to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Still requesting the full food allocation despite no date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - practical food concerns override social embarrassment. Character-specific and relatable.

jokes.text: God, three weddings in one day. I'm going to be in Spanx for 12 hours. My elastic line is going to get infected again.
jokes.setup: Complaining about wedding obligations
jokes.punchline: Escalating from minor discomfort to gross medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from fashion complaint to body horror. The specificity of 'elastic line infection' is memorably gross.

jokes.text: I will rent a car, set it on fire, and drive it off a waterfall.
jokes.setup: Not wanting to attend Floyd's wedding alone
jokes.punchline: Absurdly elaborate and dangerous alternative plan
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific, cinematic imagery. The logistics of setting a car on fire while driving it is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: He's gay. But not when he's drunk.
jokes.setup: Offering to set Liz up with her trainer
jokes.punchline: Bizarre qualifier about conditional sexuality based on alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna logic - treating sexual orientation as situational. The economy and timing are flawless, and it's exactly what this character would say.

jokes.text: Sexual time travel, just like my Cinemax softcore Emmanuelle Goes to Dinosaur Land.
jokes.setup: Explaining her plan to revisit old boyfriends
jokes.punchline: Reference to absurd softcore film that matches the episode title
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke referencing the episode title while creating a believable terrible movie title. The specificity of 'Cinemax softcore' is perfect.

jokes.text: Meet me in the handicapped stall in 10 seconds.
jokes.setup: Agreeing that Liz's standards are too high
jokes.punchline: Abrupt transition to inappropriate bathroom rendezvous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna non-sequitur. The specificity of handicapped stall and exact timing makes it funnier than generic bathroom proposition.

jokes.text: Yeah, I saw this gorgeous woman putting glasses on her daughter's Mrs. Potato Head. The why is not important.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he was thinking about Liz
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific image with acknowledgment that it makes no sense
jokes.explanation: The visual is wonderfully specific and absurd. 'The why is not important' acknowledges the absurdity perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, my hooks. I'm just so comfortable with them that I... Whoops.
jokes.setup: Drew has been talking normally, hiding his disability
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals he has hook hands by damaging something
jokes.explanation: Good physical revelation. The casual 'whoops' after destroying art with hook hands is perfectly understated.

jokes.text: That was my last ungashed painting.
jokes.setup: Drew's hooks have damaged her artwork
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is a recurring problem with very specific language
jokes.explanation: The word 'ungashed' is perfectly chosen - clinical yet evocative. Implies this happens regularly.

jokes.text: So I waved from the helicopter, which, it turns out, is a big no-no.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he lost his hand in a helicopter accident
jokes.punchline: Treating serious safety violation as mild social faux pas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement. 'Big no-no' is perfectly casual language for life-altering mistake. Shows Drew's obliviousness.

jokes.text: And it turns out the person I was waving to was not my old football coach. Of course not. You were in Zimbabwe. Well, it looked just like a black version of him.
jokes.setup: Drew explaining the mistaken identity that caused his accident
jokes.punchline: Racist logic delivered with complete obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Drew's oblivious racism. Liz's 'of course not' response is the perfect straight-man beat.

jokes.text: I've got my hooks crossed.
jokes.setup: Hoping for a hand transplant from an executed criminal
jokes.punchline: Adapting 'fingers crossed' idiom to his hook hands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels. Perfect character adaptation of common idiom, and the visual of crossed hooks is great.

jokes.text: Now, who's the dumb one? Ugh.
jokes.setup: Liz calls him dumb and leaves
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves her point by touching something hot with metal hooks
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal. Drew trying to prove his intelligence while demonstrating the opposite through physics. Great physical comedy.

jokes.text: It's a pun. Because cats' paws have grooves.
jokes.setup: Explaining the movie title 'Feline Groovy'
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining a terrible pun with incorrect cat anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - explaining something obvious while getting it wrong. The confidence in incorrect information is character-perfect.

jokes.text: And they're paying me exactly one million teachers' salaries.
jokes.setup: Describing his payment for the Garfield movie
jokes.punchline: Using teachers' salaries as unit of measurement for obscene wealth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant way to show both Tracy's wealth and his disconnect from normal people. The specificity of using teacher salaries as currency is perfect.

jokes.text: You say things like 'You don't know my pain,' and 'You watch your mouth, Tyrese,' and, in a less dramatic scene, 'I'll have hash browns.'
jokes.setup: Describing the dramatic dialogue in the Oscar-bait script
jokes.punchline: Deflating serious dramatic lines with mundane diner order
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that deflates pretentious drama with mundane reality. The careful setup with 'less dramatic scene' makes the punchline land perfectly.

jokes.text: It's either this or I submit that animated film I drew about the Holocaust.
jokes.setup: Discussing his Oscar strategy options
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of incredibly inappropriate Holocaust cartoon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy - treating the most serious historical tragedy as personal art project. The casual tone makes it funnier and more horrifying.

jokes.text: First, we're going to see a documentary about female circumcision, and then we're going to eat too much Indian food.
jokes.setup: Planning an unromantic evening to keep Nancy at arm's length
jokes.punchline: Two increasingly uncomfortable physical experiences
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from emotionally uncomfortable to physically uncomfortable. Very Jack-like strategic thinking applied to romance.

jokes.text: Sometimes, the right thing and the hard thing are the same thing. I read that on a teabag.
jokes.setup: Giving Jack profound moral advice
jokes.punchline: Revealing the source is commercial tea packaging
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - builds up profound wisdom then reveals mundane source. Perfect Liz self-awareness about her advice-giving.

jokes.text: You know what? Sometimes, I think... That's great. 'You know what? Sometimes, I think...' That's really annoying. I'm going to use that.
jokes.setup: Liz starts giving advice with her typical phrasing
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately identifies and adopts her speech pattern as an annoying tactic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about dialogue patterns. Jack's strategic thinking applied to conversational manipulation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I remember being born, of course. I remember learning how to ride a bike. But that was last year.
jokes.setup: Discussing what memories he has from childhood
jokes.punchline: Revealing he learned to ride a bike as an adult very recently
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - treating childhood development milestones as recent achievements. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I remember the summer my Webelos group went camping, and we were taken by the Hill People. Next thing I knew, summer was over and it was time for back to school shopping.
jokes.setup: Trying to remember childhood experiences
jokes.punchline: Treating apparent kidnapping by mysterious Hill People as minor summer camp incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of traumatic experience with mundane back-to-school routine. Tracy's casual delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: No, I don't. I was on a yacht with the Roots last week.
jokes.setup: Being told he needs to reconnect with his roots
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding 'roots' as the famous hip-hop band
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay that shows Tracy's literal thinking and celebrity lifestyle. The yacht detail makes it more specific and funny.

jokes.text: I've got a personalized vibration for each chick I used to put it to.
jokes.setup: Explaining how his beeper works
jokes.punchline: Gross overshare about his sexual organization system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis grossness. The technical specificity of 'personalized vibration' makes it funnier than generic crude talk.

jokes.text: No, no, no, Jose. Only babies out here. Don't you want to be a big boy?
jokes.setup: Dennis has a child mentor through some program
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate role reversal where adult needs child's guidance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of mentor relationship. Dennis needing a child to teach him basic behavior is perfectly absurd and in character.

jokes.text: What was the one mistake those people in Denver made? One mistake? Yeah, there was no kid in the balloon.
jokes.setup: Dennis referencing the balloon boy hoax
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the problem was execution, not the crime itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis criminal logic. The idea that the hoax failed due to poor planning rather than being inherently wrong is brilliantly amoral.

jokes.text: This is a public park named after Ron Artest.
jokes.setup: Liz calls his operation a scientific facility
jokes.punchline: Correcting her with absurd park naming detail
jokes.explanation: The specificity of Ron Artest (controversial NBA player) makes this funnier than generic park name. Good world-building detail.

jokes.text: Way better than the Indian joint I go to in Boston. O'Doyle's.
jokes.setup: Complimenting the Indian restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her regular Indian restaurant has an obviously Irish name
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - the Irish name O'Doyle's immediately tells us everything about Nancy's Boston experience with Indian food.

jokes.text: A warm glass of milk and some John Phillip Sousa marches.
jokes.setup: Suggesting what goes well after curry
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and unsexy combination of comfort food and patriotic music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - turning romance-killing into sophisticated personal preference. The specificity of Sousa marches makes it brilliantly unsexy.

jokes.text: No! [Liz's horrified reaction to seeing Wesley]
jokes.setup: Being introduced to her wedding table mate
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's Wesley, her most hated ex
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff to her search. The pure horror in her reaction sells the joke, though the setup is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Stop doing that. You look idiotic. Of course I do. Excellent pantomime is supposed to look idiotic.
jokes.setup: Wesley doing something visual that looks stupid
jokes.punchline: Pretentious defense that missing the point is the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wesley - turning obvious failure into artistic sophistication. The logic is circular but delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: The only thing wrong with me is that you hate me. So with that as my only defect, where do I rank with all the other men that you've been with?
jokes.setup: Acknowledging Liz hates him
jokes.punchline: Treating her hatred as a minor technical flaw rather than a fundamental problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Wesley logic - treating the core relationship problem as a minor detail. The competitive framing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: How can something that animals do be a big deal? Worms can do it with any other worm.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether sex is a big deal
jokes.punchline: Using worm reproduction as relationship model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nancy - applying practical logic to romance. The specificity of worm biology makes it funnier than generic animal comparison.

jokes.text: Also, it was after midnight on a Saturday. So technically, it was a Sunday. A man had his hands on my hips on a day that was set aside for the Lord.
jokes.setup: Confessing to dancing with a man after her divorce
jokes.punchline: Escalating guilt over technicality about Sabbath observance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Catholic guilt. The technical midnight detail and dramatic phrasing about the Sabbath are perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Man, Catholic guilt. Am I right? [Jack's awkward silence response]
jokes.setup: Nancy trying to bond over shared Catholic guilt
jokes.punchline: Jack's obvious discomfort showing he doesn't share this experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing class differences. Jack's discomfort with Nancy's working-class Catholic background creates tension.

jokes.text: I slept on an old dog bed stuffed with wigs. I watched a prostitute stab a clown.
jokes.setup: Tracy's memories returning from his childhood neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Increasingly surreal and disturbing childhood experiences
jokes.explanation: Incredibly vivid and specific imagery that's both funny and horrifying. 'Dog bed stuffed with wigs' is brilliantly specific absurdist detail.

jokes.text: Our basketball hoop was a ribcage. A ribcage!
jokes.setup: Continuing childhood poverty memories
jokes.punchline: Grotesque improvised sports equipment
jokes.explanation: Incredible visual that's both funny and disturbing. The repetition of 'A ribcage!' shows Tracy's horror. Perfectly crafted escalation.

jokes.text: Some guy with dreads electrocuted my fish!
jokes.setup: More traumatic childhood memories
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific act of pet cruelty
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with another vivid, specific detail. The specificity of 'guy with dreads' makes it feel real and absurd.

jokes.text: Nancy is a fiery, Irish nut job, descended from bog people.
jokes.setup: Comparing Nancy to Avery
jokes.punchline: Reducing Nancy to ethnic stereotypes with 'bog people' ancestry
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character comedy showing his WASP-y casual ethnic categorization

jokes.text: We're like Russ and Rebecca on Chums.
jokes.setup: Describing his relationship dynamic with Liz
jokes.punchline: Reference to obvious Friends parody show
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wesley's pretentious cultural references. 'Chums' is obvious Friends parody that fits the show's meta humor.

jokes.text: Did you see the Beijing opening ceremonies? We don't have control over our people like that.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't return to England for Olympics
jokes.punchline: Envying authoritarian control for event planning
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about Olympics logistics with dark political undertones. Wesley's casual acceptance of authoritarianism is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Nermal, I hate you, Nermal! Almost as much as I hate Mondays! This is my lasagna! You hear me, Nermal? My lasagna!
jokes.setup: Tracy doing Garfield voice work
jokes.punchline: Bringing intense personal trauma to cartoon cat dialogue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Tracy can't separate his trauma from cartoon work. The intensity brings real emotion to silly dialogue.

jokes.text: A crack-head breastfeeding a rat. A homeless man cooking a Hot Pocket on the third rail of the G train. The G train, Nermal!
jokes.setup: Tracy breaking character during Garfield filming
jokes.punchline: More specific traumatic memories with train line specificity
jokes.explanation: Incredible escalation with vivid imagery. The G train detail is perfectly specific, and addressing Nermal makes it surreal.

jokes.text: I'm a plushie. Is that a fraternity? Kind of. It means I belong to a group of like-minded people who dress up in mascot costumes. And have orgies in hotel rooms and state parks.
jokes.setup: Seemingly normal guy reveals unusual hobby
jokes.punchline: Innocent costume hobby escalates to sexual fetish
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure. Starts innocent, builds through explanation, then hits with disturbing sexual element. Liz's innocent question helps build.

jokes.text: Our term for intercourse is 'yiffing'.
jokes.setup: Already established the sexual nature of plushie community
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specific terminology makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the already weird situation weirder. The term 'yiffing' is real furry terminology, adding authenticity.

jokes.text: Speaking of which, I'm seeing someone else, and I think I'm in love with both of you.
jokes.setup: Jack's guilt about being in church
jokes.punchline: Confessing his love triangle during a wedding ceremony
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe escalation with perfect timing and inappropriate honesty

jokes.text: You can't leave, Donovan. We're in the middle of Mass. You and I both know you have to stay until the final blessing.
jokes.setup: Nancy wants to leave after Jack's revelation
jokes.punchline: Using Catholic rules to trap someone in uncomfortable confrontation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant manipulation using religious obligation. Jack weaponizing Catholic guilt against Nancy is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he lay with his brother's wife, he spilled his semen on the ground.
jokes.setup: Liz doing an 'appropriate' Bible reading at a wedding
jokes.punchline: Reading explicit sexual content about contraception at romantic occasion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inappropriate Bible selection. Liz's attempt to help Jack creates maximum awkwardness with religious sexual content.

jokes.text: Then, Zipporah took a flint and cut off her son's foreskin. Oh come on, Bible. Help a lady out.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to find appropriate Bible passages
jokes.punchline: Finding even worse content and directly addressing the Bible
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from sexual content to violence. Breaking the fourth wall with 'Help a lady out' is brilliant desperation.

jokes.text: I seen a blind guy bite a police horse! A puppy committed suicide after he saw our bathroom!
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing his traumatic neighborhood memories
jokes.punchline: Increasingly surreal and impossible scenarios
jokes.explanation: Incredible escalation with vivid, impossible imagery. 'Puppy committed suicide' is darkly hilarious and perfectly Tracy.

jokes.text: I once bit into a Merino and there was a child's shoe in it!
jokes.setup: Continuing the traumatic food experiences
jokes.punchline: Impossible food contamination with specific cookie brand
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with specific brand reference making it funnier. The impossibility of fitting a shoe in a cookie is perfect.

jokes.text: A pack of wild dogs took over and successfully ran a Wendy's!
jokes.setup: More neighborhood chaos
jokes.punchline: Dogs not just causing trouble but succeeding at business management
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - dogs not just destroying but improving the business. 'Successfully' is the key word that makes this perfect.

jokes.text: The projects I lived in was named after Zachary Taylor, generally considered to be one of the worst presidents of all time!
jokes.setup: More childhood environment details
jokes.punchline: Even the inspirational naming was depressingly appropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect bitter irony - even the inspirational naming was a failure. The historical specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: The sewer people stole my skateboard!
jokes.setup: Tracy's traumatic property theft
jokes.punchline: Mythical underground dwellers as petty thieves
jokes.explanation: Good continuation with mythical urban creatures doing mundane crimes

jokes.text: I once saw a baby give another baby a tattoo! They were very drunk!
jokes.setup: Final traumatic childhood memory
jokes.punchline: Impossible scenario made worse by additional impossible detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax to the escalation. The image is impossible but delivered with complete conviction. 'They were very drunk!' is the perfect capper.
